title: FDA warns against use of two-dozen eye drop products sold at CVS, Rite Aid over risk of infection and vision loss
text: The FDA is telling consumers to avoid two dozen eye drop products sold at major retailers including CVS and Rite Aid, because they could cause eye infections and potentially lead to vision loss.

title: CVS, Target and other eye-drop products pose risk of dangerous infections, FDA warns
text: More than two dozen over-the-counter eye-drop products marketed under CVS Health Corp. CVS, +1.40%, Target Corp. TGT, +1.15%, Rite Aid Corp. RADCQ, +8.91% and other brands pose a risk of eye infections that could cause partial vision loss or blindness, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration warned Friday.

title: Strike Alert 2023: Workers at CVS, Walgreens, Rite Aid Walk Out
text: Workers at pharmacy companies CVS (NYSE: CVS ), Walgreens (NASDAQ: WBA ) and RiteAid (OTCMKTS: RADCQ ) have staged a strike that has them walking out on the businesses for three days to highlight working conditions The strikes at CVS, Walgreens and RiteAid are focused on unsafe working conditions. Employees of these companies are focusing on how understaffed locations are, requiring a daunting amount of work for the company's workers.

title: CVS Health to remove cough medicines due to effectiveness doubts
text: CVS Health said yesterday it is to remove oral cough and cold products with phenylephrine as the only active ingredient from its shelves following growing concerns over the drug's effectiveness. Phenylephrine is widely used in decongestant products with brands such as Benadryl and Tylenol said to contain the drug.

title: CVS pulls popular cold medicines from store shelves
text: CVS said on Thursday that it is voluntarily removing certain common oral cough and cold products after a Food and Drug Administration (FDA) advisory panel determined last month that its active ingredient doesn't work.

title: CVS to pull certain cold medicines containing decongestant phenylephrine from store shelves
text: The FDA has not decided whether to ask drug manufacturers and retailers like CVS to remove products containing oral phenylephrine from the market.

title: CVS voluntarily pulling decongestants from shelves that contain ineffective ingredient
text: CVS Health Corp. CVS, +0.14% is pulling decongestants off their shelves that contain an ingredient an Food and Drug Administration advisory committee determined did not work, according to a Thursday report from the Wall Street Journal. Used as a key ingredient in decongestants for nearly a century, FDA advisors in September determined that phenylephrine did not improve congestion.

title: CVS Health to remove cold medicine with phenylephrine from shelves - WSJ
text: CVS Health is pulling some of the most common decongestants with phenylephrine from its shelves and will no longer sell them, after advisers to U.S. health regulators raised doubt over the efficacy of the ingredient, according to a Wall Street Journal report on Thursday.
